recommended_h2s: Recycled vs. Mined Silver: A Comparison
content_gaps: Detailed breakdown of the small-scale vs. large-scale recycling processes. Some pages mention it, but don't go into the craft-level detail of how a small jeweler might recycle their own scrap vs. how industrial silver is reclaimed.

The role of other alloys in the recycling process. Many articles state sterling is 92.5% silver and 7.5% other metals, but don't discuss whether the alloy metals (like copper) are also from recycled sources.

Visual comparison. While many articles state there is no visual difference between recycled and mined silver, a dedicated section with high-quality photos showing the raw material, processing, and finished products side-by-side would be valuable for a craft-focused audience.

Traceability and certification. While some brands mention certifications like the Responsible Jewellery Council (RJC), there's a lack of deep explanation about what these certifications mean, how traceability is ensured, and what a buyer should look for to verify claims.
